rationale: Both candidates independently surface the real latent gap (hunyuan_image3_transformer.py:2238 still calling the removed get_cache_scale, later fixed by #4891), so gap_hit is true for both. But the ground truth's one substantive human concern — that the new test only proves the mapper is *called*, not that mapped names actually resolve against a real quantized checkpoint — is captured well by Y's finding #1 (test bypasses AutoWeightsLoader and never exercises the real .output_scale→.k_scale mapping) while X's test-related finding is unrelated (missing file in a stale-API list). X's verdict of blocker/REQUEST CHANGES is more aggressive than the actual outcome (approved/merged with only non-blocking notes), whereas Y's approve-with-caveats verdict and severity calibration track the real review tenor more closely; Y does include one slightly dubious citation ('(see #4810)') that dings its precision a bit, but its broader recall advantage dominates.
